200.description: Chat completion response. Returns JSON for standard requests and server-sent events when `stream=true`.

schema.description: Non-streaming chat completion response.

created.description: Unix timestamp in seconds when the completion was created.

model.description: Model used to generate the completion.

items.description: One completion choice returned by the model.

index.description: Zero-based index of this choice in the response.

role.description: Role of the generated message author.

content.description: Text content generated by the assistant.

finish_reason.description: Reason the model stopped generating tokens.

prompt_tokens.description: Number of prompt tokens consumed by the request.

completion_tokens.description: Number of tokens generated in the completion.

total_tokens.description: Total tokens used across prompt and completion.

schema.description: Server-sent event stream of chat completion chunks.

error.description: Error details for the failed request.
Error details for the failed request.
Error details for the failed request.

schema.description: Parameters for creating a chat completion.

model.description: Identifier of the model to use for the completion.

role.description: Role of the author of this message.

stream.description: If true, the response is streamed as server-sent events.

temperature.description: Sampling temperature to use for generation.

max_tokens.description: Maximum number of tokens to generate in the completion.

top_p.description: Nucleus sampling value used as an alternative to temperature.

stop.description: Sequence or sequences that cause generation to stop when encountered.

frequency_penalty.description: Penalty applied to tokens based on how often they already appear in the text.

presence_penalty.description: Penalty applied to tokens based on whether they already appear in the text.

meta.description: Set of key-value pairs that you can attach to the request for your own bookkeeping.

schema.description: Parameters for creating a brain session.

model.description: Runtime model identifier for the selected brain environment.

mode.description: Permission mode used by the brain runtime.

max_cycles.description: Maximum number of execution cycles before the brain stops itself.

training.description: Enable training-specific prompts when the server allows training mode.

neuron_policy.description: Optional denylist policy that blocks matching neuron key prefixes.

mode.description: Neuron policy mode. Only `denylist` is currently supported.

denied_prefixes.description: Neuron key prefixes that should be blocked for this brain.

url.description: URL where the generated image can be retrieved.

b64_json.description: Base64-encoded image data when the provider returns inline content.

storage.description: Persisted storage location for the promoted asset.

content_type.description: Detected content type for the stored asset.

asset_id.description: Layerbrain asset identifier for the promoted object.

error.description: Error details for the failed request.
Error details for the failed request.
Error details for the failed request.

schema.description: Image edit request.

prompt.description: Text prompt describing the desired image.

negative_prompt.description: Text describing what to avoid in the image.

output.description: Optional explicit output naming for persisted assets.

directory.description: Bucket-root-relative directory where generated assets should be stored.

name.description: Base file name stem. The system derives the final extension.

meta.description: Set of key-value pairs that you can attach to the request for your own bookkeeping.

post.description: Runs a web search and returns the normalized results that Layerbrain stores for the request.

error.description: Error details for the failed request.
Error details for the failed request.

schema.description: Web search response payload.

results.description: Search results returned for the query.

items.description: One search result returned by the web search tool.

title.description: Title of the search result page.

url.description: Canonical URL for the search result.

snippet.description: Short excerpt describing the page content.

total.description: Number of results returned in this response.

schema.description: Parameters for a web search request.

count.description: Maximum number of results to return.

allowed_domains.description: Optional list of domains to include in the search.

blocked_domains.description: Optional list of domains to exclude from the search.

date_range.description: Optional date range filter applied by the search provider.

safesearch.description: Safe search level applied to the query.

freshness.description: Optional freshness filter such as `pd`, `pw`, `pm`, or `py`.

meta.description: Set of key-value pairs that you can attach to the request for your own bookkeeping.

schema.description: Fields used to create an outbound webhook endpoint.

url.description: HTTPS endpoint that will receive signed event payloads.

enabled_events.description: Event names delivered to this webhook. Use ["*"] to receive all emitted events.

description.description: Optional human-readable description for the endpoint.

data.description: Optional arbitrary structured data stored with the webhook.

meta.description: Optional metadata stored with the webhook.

x-layerbrain-websocket.description: Stream webhook events for the authenticated organization over WebSocket.

parameters.name: events
parameters.description: Comma-separated event types to stream. Omit to receive all events.

schema.description: Mutable webhook fields.

url.description: Updated HTTPS endpoint for webhook delivery.

enabled_events.description: Event names delivered to this webhook. Use ["*"] to receive all emitted events.

status.description: Enabled or disabled state for the endpoint.

data.description: Updated arbitrary structured data stored with the webhook.

meta.description: Updated metadata stored with the webhook.
